title: Use "while" loop with "Object.wait(...)" and "Condition.await(...)"
description: In a multithreaded environment, the Object.wait(...) and Condition.await(...) methods are used to pause the execution of a thread. However, a thread may be awakened despite the desired condition not being met or the desired event not having happened. This rule suggests using a "while" loop to ensure the desired condition is met before continuing.
recommendation: Wrap Object.wait(...) and Condition.await(...) calls in a "while" loop that checks the desired condition.
impact: Prevent spurious wakeups and ensure correct execution of multithreaded code.

checks.message: Wrap Object.wait(...) and Condition.await(...) calls in a "while" loop that checks the desired condition.
